channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS… — 61 replies — 229.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS…

Open thread • Replies: 61 • Updated: 2026-06-18 04:41 UTC

OP

NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!!

THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!!

SOMEONE MUST PUT A STOP TO THIS NOW!!!!!!!!!!!! REDDIT ARMY RISE UP!!!!!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62375140
this is entirely sustainable, you have EDS if you disagree

62375150
>>62375140
Short it then, stupid nigger

62375163
>company called "Space Exploration Technologies"
>run by the first trillionaire

>you STILL didn't buy any of their stock

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62375898
Give Elon more rupees saars!
Plenty of Bob and Vagene awaits!

62375943
>>62375776
so true sis, now let's see the mcap on airliners

62376171
>>62375128
>ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN
True
>SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!!
Also true

62376208
We'll see how much a memestock it is when a Super Heavy explodes, next launch is in July

62376220
>>62375128
Please post some reddit salt

62376256
>>62375128
Thank you for reminding me to buy some etf shorts lol

62376261
>>62376256
stupid nigger, SPCX is worth $14T. new target price is roughly $500 and that's conservative.

62376313
>>62376261
I'm shorting times 2 now. You happy yet?

62376334
>>62375128
Space X is Mooning to Mars!! Elon said he'd get us there

62376337
>>62375128
Are goys allowed to sell yet?

62376364
>>62375755
Satellites alone would be worth multiple trillions.

62376848
shorted at 220

feels good man

62377060
>>62375393
Whatever nigger, he kept promising the Hyperloop, the robotaxis and Mars for next year since 2015.

>"But buh reddit"

Curious how Elon was the guy being praised by Onions individuals in reddit before he allied with Trump, now he is praised by le based onions individuals like you.

62377191
>>62375818
SpaceX isn’t even a space company. It’s an AI company (and underperforming shit AI) with a rocket hobby. You also only get access to 4% of the stock on retail. You’re begging for a giga rugpull.

62377238
>>62375128
AND A ZIONIST!!

62377697
>>62375128
hm, congrats to the buyers.

62379451
>>62375140
mathematically impossible valuation.

62379484
>>62375255
Your instinctive contrarianism is why you belong to the permanent underclass

62379507
>>62375841
Anti-natalists are mentally ill and project their retardation onto others.

62379519
>>62377191
tbf I like grok more than gemini, copilot, or deepseek. Not sure yet how I think it stacks up to claude or chatgpt though.

62381795
>>62375140
how to gotted so much dollars?

62381800
>>62375128
Imagine believing 2 weeks represents absolutely anything about the viability or future trajectory of a stock. No wonder you guys get annihilated so regularly.

62381830
>>62377191
SpaceX performs 60% of the world's space launches, which is 95% of the American market. If they aren't a space company, then there isn't a space company that exists at all.

62381991
>>62381830
>He thinks (((outer space))) is like in the movies and what they teach in (((schools))) and show on your (((television)))
Cute. You need to be 18 to post here, champ.

62382075
>>62375140
>>62375255
Unlike faggy AI, space is an actual next frontier of human development.
While indians are busy generating piles of poo here on earth to jerk off to, real MEN will be mining asteroids in space and speculating on the Asteroid Belt stock exchange

62382118
my parents aped in and told me they didn't want to get left behind lol. Load zee 60% dump.

62382338
>>62382075
>real MEN will be mining asteroids

62382719
>>62382075
>Unlike faggy AI, space is an actual next frontier of human development.

SpaceX won't fucking EXIST by the time we get there.

62382732
>>62382118
That's really sad :(

62382961
>>62379484

item.title: There are levels to this — 32 replies — 128.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are levels to this

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-18 04:48 UTC

OP

https://x.com/i/status/2067036752762847631

>intelligent, hard working, cosmopolitan women
>making over $300k in sales in New York

>typical 4channer
>limp dick racist making $45k in HVAC maintenance in Pawtucket, Missouri

Yeah, college is a scam, or something...

Early replies (first 3)

62381592
all so ugly why would any1 hire them

i know they are all jewish because why else would some1 hire such ugly women

62381660
>He isn’t racist

Why are you brown?

62381685
>>62381592
>>62381660
Stop bumping 0/10 threads, pls and thank you

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62381686
4chansisters, what's our response

62381704
>>62381543
what's the point of "making over $300k" if you're a retarded foid spending more than that
>$7000 rent
kek

62381736
>>62381543
>make 300k in new york
>live like a bug in the rented pod
>walk down the street because car ownership is impossible
>make 45k in pawtucket
>own a fuckoff big house on your own land
>own two cars a truck and five motorbikes
hard choice

62381837
96% of all new jobs created are now going to women btw.

62381860
>>62381543
Making $300k in New York is like making $45k in Pawtucket

62381863
>>62381543
Salespeople typically don't go to college anon.
Women get any jobs they want 300k starting even without any qualification by the way. It's 2026.

62381866
>>62381543
>Pawtucket, Missouri

62381882
>>62381543
who is hiring these women? they're not even eye candy tier but have ego's higher than low iq eye candy

62381889
>>62381592
Phenotypes check out
Obvious Chosen folx

62381890
>>62381882
Uggos and fatsos always have much higher egos than eye candy because their "peers" are handed jobs for free, so they feel that (by comparison) they have worked so unbelievably hard for the job they got (and all other things in life). They're also the ones that always go "muh patriarchy" unironically not realizing that 90% of men would kill to have even a fraction of the advantages they've received in life.

62381893
I bet 300 shekels I could do their jobs with a few days of training at most. But alas I'm a working class cracker so no dice

62381993
>>62381736
>pawtucket
you wanna meet up?

62382011
>>62381543
im just a humble neet chad. i don't get the appeal of the lifestyle these women have. all life they grind to look like fuckdolls and consume whatever their social media tells them to but beyond that its pretty empty in there

62382018
>>62381543
Fake adult day care job designed solely to preoccupy women from reproducing, employed by a company that produces very little if anything of actual value, kept afloat by a fake economy fueled by fake money and unpayable credit.

When this finally crashes it's going to crash hard

62382298
>>62381543
How does the average woman outside of NY fare?

62382582
>>62381543
i can't get a meme jew daycare job

62382600
>>62381543
>young

62382711
>>62381897
>Daily Mail
Nice try

62382722
>>62381543
Making 220-300k plus in Manhattan or the bay area sets your 401k up for life since you're matching 12% each year. If you buy a condo you can sell it when you're 29 and buy a million dollar home wherever you want

62382739
>>62381736
/thread

clean it up jannies

62382740
>>62381543
You sound like a gay retard, good luck with your 300k sales job thats totally real and is totally necessary. Should do well as the entire nation breaks down over the next few years

62382748
>>62382711
doesn't matter if its reuters or ap or new york times or daily mail, no such thing as trustworthy journalism. if you think otherwise you're naive. at least daily mail doesn't pretend to be anything it isn't, it knows its slop. the best most important stories no one else will cover next to the most trashy vapid nonsense. leftoid retards and right wing schizos alike publishing articles. daily mail is pure chaos and from that chaos sometimes the truths others won't tell.

62382830
>>62381863
Where’s my state sanctioned 300k job? I’d fucking love that

62382875
>>62381543
So you are telling me i can raise their rent even more? That's nice.

62382896
>>62381543
>mfw after I make 25 dollars after shoveling snow for 6 hours

62382969
>>62382896
You should check that rc car that she gave you, maybe there's a military chip inside.

item.title: You don't need that new car. You don't need a car from the dealership let alone multiple leases. You don't need a 2k mor… — 31 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You don't need that new car. You don't need a car from the dealership let alone multiple leases. You don't need a 2k mor…

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-18 04:52 UTC

OP

You don't need that new car. You don't need a car from the dealership let alone multiple leases. You don't need a 2k mortgage. You don't need plane tickets when a road trip can save money. You don't need all those subscriptions. You don't need to hire landscapers. You don't need apple products. You don't need student loans. You don't need a smart fridge. You don't need fancy food for your dog. You don't need a 4k tv. You don't need to go skiing. You don't need a boat. You don't need to eat at restaurants. You don't need a gaming PC. You don't need to pay more than $30 for a haircut. Or anything really. You don't need to shop at Sam's club. You don't need to shop at Costco. You don't need to shop at Best Buy. You don't need to buy a bunch of fancy ingredients and larp as a chef. You don't need a granite counter top. You don't need to buy Claude tokens. You don't need to buy sports tickets. You don't need a cat. You don't need to pay more than $35 for a phone bill. You don't need to pay for new tires. You can get them used. You don't need to buy premium gas. You don't need to tell the insurance company how many people live with you. You don't need to buy any books, use project Gutenberg. You don't need all those tools in the garage. You don't use half of them anyways. You don't need a home with a garage. You don't need to be on the HOA. You don't need to go on weekly dates with your gf. You don't need to go on weekly dates with your wife. You don't need to buy condoms. You don't need to buy diapers. You don't need all sorts of sauces and condiments in the fridge. You don't need warranties ANY warranty, just take care of your things. You don't need to go to concerts. You don't need to have a blue checkmark. You don't need to eat food at events. You don't need a house with stairs. You don't need carpet on those stairs. You don't need a leather couch. You don't need to have the fastest wifi. You don't need to buy pornographic art. You don't need to buy beer. Or any drug.

Early replies (first 3)

62377958
>>62377955
man no one is reading allat nigga fucking die in a fire.

62378430
some wealthy people have so much money they don't even have enough time to spend it. the costs of things is just another thing that takes time. the secret is to remove the things that gatekeep time so the time can be spent on things you want not have to

62378534
>>62377955
I don't know what you meant by this but this was the most relaxing thing I've ever read and it describes my life and outlook

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62378550
>>62377955
I needed this, thank you

62378563
>>62377955
>You don't need to buy condoms
Smile crept onto my face. Based

62378597
>>62377955
you don't need to live kys faggot

62378610
>>62377958
this.
only thing people need to read is the fact I need 10k.

62378617
>>62377955
I'm not reading that wall of text.

I already have a new car (Toyota Corolla) paid off. My rent is $900/mo and I split it with my mom, so it's really only $450. I don't like sitting in a car for hours or driving on a freeway. A flight to Miami from where I live is about 2.5 hours, a car ride would be 19 hours if you don't stop for food, restroom, gas or sleep.

62378622
>>62378617
no one asked

62378624
>>62377955
I do want some of these things however, and I will use money to buy them. What, am I supposed to live miserably like some FIRE redditor and spend the best years of my life physically on wage slaving for a future that may never come or that I will be too damaged to enjoy? No thanks.

62378680
>>62378622
ESL?

Learn proper grammar, retard.

62378689
>>62378680
you might want to go back to school if you think that this isn't proper grammar, nigger

62378695
>>62377955
I need the cute and funny tho.

62378857
based op

62378869
>>62378689
>He still doesn't capitalize the first letter, or end a comment with a period.

62378877
>>62378869
this has nothing to do with proper grammar or ESL. Nobody types like this any longer, fucking boomer

62378880
>>62378877
Don't text me until you're educated enough to have an intellectual conversation.

62378883
>>62378880
take the L, grandpa

62378886
>>62378883
*Accept you have lost, senior citizen.

62378888
>>62378886
bro, just say you have asperger, you already gave it away the moment you said you live with your mom

62378897
>>62378888
Quads don't lie...

62378926
>>62377958
why do you speak like a little nigger from the jungle ?

62379963
>>62377955
tell that to the communist invaders who are actually materialists

62379983
>>62377955
>You don't need to shop at Sam's club. You don't need to shop at Costco.
but those places save you money...

62380058
yeah, and commies should get shot at the head and get hanged on electric poles with their guts out

62380088
>>62377955
I need all of that + more, so fuck you and fork it over porky *shoves gun in your face*

62380106
>>62377955
>You don't need to pay more than $35 for a phone bill
>$35
thats way too much. 700pc overpriced. mint mobile is $5/mo.
get on my level.

62380264
>>62380106
>Mint mobile - $15/mo
Get on my level, Tello mobile.
$10/ account .
I already rolled over 3 numbers there that I don't need. And have 1 extra backup phone and phone line.

62382946
>>62380264
>>62380106
ENTER
https://www.ebay.com/itm/136840233242

62382962
>>62377955
You are right OP. Every single company providing these goods and services should go broke from loss of revenue. All wagies need to lose their jobs.

62382977
>>62377955
I need my 2k a month mortgage or i will lose my house. Also, i'm not getting rid of my cats.

item.title: How is your career going? — 38 replies — 103.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is your career going?

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-06-18 04:35 UTC

OP

I make $28/hr as a Fire Alarm Inspection Technician. I have 2 licenses and 4.5 years of experience:
-FASA/BASA
-NICET Inspection and Testing Level 2

Early replies (first 3)

62381055
Going for the NICET Fire Alarm Systems Level 1 license in the next 2 months or so. Will hopefully get to $30/hr.

62381060
>>62381049
*chirp*

62381078
>>62381049
is that a fun job? sounds comfy desu

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382150
>>62381049
Bro needs a liocense to listen for a *chirp* XD

62382153
>>62381113
>Job security is there since it's regulated infrastructure and needs to be maintained/inspected year round.
surely that regulation will never change

62382160
>>62381049
I MAKE $60 AN HOUR CUMMING ON MILF TITTIES

62382172
We had a fire alarm test a work. A 3 story office building. As soon as the sirens went off a sprinkler let go in the cafeteria. We had to work from home for like 3 weeks. It was great! What causes this? I though the sprinkler only goes off if it had heat.

62382184
>>62381049
Im a NEET but I did a typing test yesterday and suddenly feel like becoming a court reporter would be a comfy job desu
Too bad it's apparently not even correlated to WPM on a normal keyboard

62382261
>>62382172
Fire alarms systems are separate from sprinkler systems for the most part. There are certain sprinkler devices that interact with the fire alarm system, but generally if a sprinkler goes off, it's because someone broke the bulb on it, or the heat caused the liquid in the bulb to expand and break it.

62382290
>>62382261
Hmmm you made me think more about it. The one that went off was on the second floor, but directly below it was the main entrance. I wonder if that one is controlled by the alarm so people can use the main entrance as an exit if there is a fire.

62382303
>>62381049
Weekly hours? Opportunities for part time at that rate?

62382307
uhh i make $23 an hour but its salaried with decent vacation and low social interaction

62382353
>>62381049
terrible im unemployed for 7 months and cant get interviews at best buy

62382431
>>62382303
Anywhere from 35-40 hours with overtime coming regularly during some stretches of the year. As far as I know there is no part-time role.

62382434
>>62382290
Sadly, as cool as that would be,you can't control which sprinkler goes off unless you light a lighter under it, or break it with a hammer haha

62382436
>>62382184
Pretty comfy, but its closer to learning an instrument and you might have to sit in court all day depending on what you are doing. Not a court reporter though. Also the equipment is kind of pricey. But, you can get deals on student gear.

62382484
>>62381049
In like 1973 you’d be based. You are a fag now.

62382503
>>62382484
Why 1973?

62382522
>>62381049
I make over 90k as an IT Manager for a multi-state company with around 180 employees. No certs, no degree. They were a companies client I worked for and they were impressed with my work. You and I are not the same.

62382530
>>62382522
How did you get started?

62382539
>>62381049
career?

62382549
180k operations manager. Adult babysitter. Plan to be retired in 5 years if investments keep going the right way, sick of this shit.

62382558
I make ~$30-50/hr CAD delivering pizza depending on the shift, but I top out at 27 hours a week.

62382757
I inspect different warehouses for inventory of different stock. Basically just write numbers on a count sheet and then submit it. Get paid 100k/yr plus I got 160k in stocks in tax free accounts.

I guess it beats micromanaged cubicle white collared work and back breaking blue collared labor. Just gonna do this until I can retire in my mid 40s

62382779
Not great
> I used to be a great coder with bad soft skills
> AI has made soft skills more important than coding
> I hate sitting in front of a computer all day but it pays at least 3X what I could get in a more physical job

62382786
>>62381049
i'm a software engineer, fucking hate it because i got promoted beyond the point of writing code. i actually love writing code and i just want to build shit, but now that's no longer my job and it's pretty fucking gay all around

62382789
>>62382786
Writing code is extremely boring with Claude anyway, and refusing to use it is not an option

62382806
35 an hour doing monkey data entry (accounts payable) at a hospital. I have a bachelors in accounting and this is my first entry level job. Before this I worked at an Amazon warehouse. Took me a whole 5 years to get an office job. I barely tried though so it’s my fault
t. 28

62382818
>>62381049
I've been stuck in public service for 12 years doing work I hate. I recommended my coworker who turned out to be a complete fucking idiot once he started working here. Hes going to get us both fired. I have missed out on better opportunities here because I am too nice and didn't advocate for myself. Its all so tiresome.

62382822
>>62381049
Have you identified which government regulations secure your job?
Seems like an important thing to know.

62382877
>>62381049
27m, 150k, fully remote. on track to become senior and make ~180k

swe

62382899
>>62381049
24m
$45/hr as a Information Management/Technical Consultant.
Been at it for 4 years:
-Mostly just showing people how to organize data and operate basic proprietary corpo programs.
-90% of the work is physical (walking in, seeing something has obviously been done wrong, fixing it)
Job security is that people will always have trouble with the machines they use on a day to day basis. People will always be stupid and mess things up.

>>62382184
On the equipment side, stenotype's have been around since the 1910's so you can easily get comfortable with the layout on a typewriter from the 1950's for $30.

>>62382303
>Weekly hours?
It's rural, my usual is roughly 30 hours. I get calls from 100+ miles away who are willing to pay enough to have me come out and teach.
A lot of the extra dough comes from just claiming what the company wants to through out tech wise, then wiping and reselling their entire server (made in 2021) online or in person through third party marketplaces. Make about double what the hourly is with that.

>>62382530
>how did you get started
I joke to a lot of the upper class clients "no one hired me, so I hired myself"

>>62382877
Is it niche work? How do you prevent your job from getting jeeted out?

62382947
>>62382899
>Is it niche work? How do you prevent your job from getting jeeted out?

we have some foreign contractors but we have a shit ton of funding covering burn so we can afford to hire citizens. it's not a unique business but we're niche in our approach and field (data/ai). we're actually hiring right now.

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 51 replies — 66.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-06-18 04:11 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62375554
>>62372051
Maybe, but doesn't the ETF route introduce a different trade-off?

62375717
Nice triple post faglord

62376610
>>62372056
No reason to stop printing

62376672
>>62376610

62377200
>>62372468
Nft lol

62377213
>>62372915
What do you mean?

62377217
>>62374451
How do you do that?

62377221
Ethereum

62377225
>>62372419
Link is fine. Wait till $1000

62377236
>>62371718
Not a BTC investor myself, but I imagine it's hard to ignore something that has repeatedly survived the kinds of events many thought would kill it.

62377444
Nothing about bitcoin has fundamentally changed.

62377533
>>62377444
C-checked

62377691
>>62377444
>Nothing about bitcoin has fundamentally changed.
checked and confirmed

62378619
>>62377217
You can natively stake it through a protocol while still maintaining self-custody. That way, you can earn more instead of just holding your BTC.

62378621
>>62372059
Maybe he's wrong. Maybe he's right. Either way, locking in gains after buying near the lows is hard to criticize.

62378868
>>62378619
If you're doing on centralised services you're cooked

62378871
>>62377691
Genius nerd. pretentious

62378977
>>62377225
go see a doctor

62379055
>>62377221
Long or short?

62379100
>>62373685
inflation is real

62380423
>>62378868
I didn't say that. Check my comment again. And if you look into TBVs, you'll probably get a better sense of what I mean.

62380435
>>62372189
You bullish or not?

62380445
>>62378868
This bot talks about bitcoin staking in every conversation on the subject, I concluded that it was a scam and should be avoided like the plague.

62380727
>>62372915
>Attack Bitcoin with 51% and try to break the chain
>Devs implement a temporary reversible limit to increase attacker costs
"Boohoo now we can't afford to attack it" kiss Bitcoin's ass ya bitch.

62381254
>>62380727
>>>>fuck off to other sha function, or mergemine, retards
>>>NO!
>>ok fine
>>*gets 99% of sha256 hashrete*
>omg they got 99% of sha256 hashrate!!!! quick, lets give up on PoW!!! we are the real BTC btc
kek

62381740
>>62381254
>Nigger can't even into PoW
Why would Bitcoin need to change it's PoW? Tell me where in the whitepaper this is suggested as a means to defend the original chain from a malicious scam fork.
>The real BTC
BTC doesn't even have a whitepaper and certainly cannot claim relation to the software described by Satoshi.

62382616
>>62379055
Your choice lol

62382799
>>62381254
You mean Bitcoin doesn't have any usecases?

62382804
>>62381740
Are you serious nigger?

62382895
buying when boomers capitulate and then riding it all the way to the top must be one of the best feelings ever, but somehow i always end up not buying because I always get lured by bears into waiting for "bettery entry prices".

item.title: Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology — 58 replies — 57.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-06-18 03:56 UTC

OP

This will be one of the greatest investment opportunities in our lifetime within another decade or so.

Tech is stagnating, just as every other industry is out of viable optimization methodologies. There is nothing new being created, every niche is saturated and optimized. AI will produce some value but it is ultimately a giant bubble that will never, and I mean never advance to the point of consciousness or intelligence (unless they use biological brains and do so correctly - they will fail). Science doesn't even know where consciousness comes.

That being said: sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness) will be the big new technology that solves real world problems. And the funny thing is: tech companies will eventually run out of other means of generating increasing revenue and will essentially HAVE to go this route, just like they're currently doing with AI. They have nothing else. This technology will have vast and continuous demand as it will entirely replace the old/current paradigm.

Early replies (first 3)

62375864
>>62375553
I want sexbots to be real so bad

62375914
>>62375864
same
all the despair, pain and unhappiness i feel... if it was a good sexbot i would be happy.
the thing is, the first sexbots will be bad. like truly awful. probably not physically dangerous... maybe. but not enjoyable. like how electric masturbators are really good at making me nut, but a soulless experience that leaves me feeling empty. the technology to replace a living organism with a robot is just not there.
if it were viable, there would be news. most people who are lonely and horny want the sexbot. but porn and masturbators are the best we have. sexbots are an AI complete problem.

62375977
>>62375553
>sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness)
AI is not about creating consciousness. you are retarded if you think intelligence requires consciousness.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62380593
>>62379051
kys

62380729
>>62375553
>artificial womb tech
This will be medtech, so unless you're an insider with knowledge of what's going to get approved, you'll never pick the winner for that.
>sex robots
Whoever seems poised to make the best of these though (and that should be easier to predict since there won't immediately be any regulation for them), I agree that will be a make it play.

62380758
>>62376962
Human beings are largely microbial networks and there are unknown epigenetic factors involved in pregnancy and childbirth as well, both which depend heavily on the mother, both with long-lasting effects on the child (likelihood of future disease, probably autism, probably all kinds of things). If we don't fully understand this now we won't be able to replicate it in an artificial environment.

62380759
>>62380729
whoever comes up with the best method of creating synthetic human flesh and reducing the uncanny valley relative to the others. I assume they will all use AI integration to produce speech etc.

62380788
>>62380759
I honestly would never use one though. The privacy issues would be nightmarish unless you had Musk tier wealth and could build/buy one solely for your own usage and jailbroken to use some locally hosted model for speech/"cognition".

62380948
>>62380788
>I honestly would never use one though
the only way I would consider using one is if you literally couldn't tell it wasn't a real human which won't happen in my lifetime, or possibly ever. They would basically have to be bladerunner level replicants for me.

62380953
>>62380788
i honestly dont care if SV nerds are measuring my vitals.
if the sexbot is good i will use it.

62380972
>>62380948
thats the problem. robotics is not even close to being solved, and the best ones sre heavy as fuck. we are still basically in the stone age. fucking disney, an entertainment company was the world leader in robotics for years and this was the best they could do.

62382444
>>62380322
the word you mean is "sentience". you are also below average iq.

62382448
>>62375914
People will eventually realise that sexbots are a flash in the pan because sex is not about getting off. If that was the case porn would be enough. You could watch porn and wank yourself silly and pretend you're having sex with the woman. But it leaves you feeling empty because your brain knows on a deep down level that you are not actually sharing the experience with someone, the oxytocin chemicals do not get released, and its void of happiness. You feel pleasure, but you arent happy.

Hookers are far superior to sex robots and are probably cheaper.

Sex robots are hookers. I dont understand how people think sex robots will be the future when hookers are literally right there and are real women and will have sex with you for money. Like a sex robot, except its a real person.

62382450
>>62379611
yes but greeks didn't have technology that is buck breaking us.

women are natural chaotic evil. they exemplify the bestial nature of man.

men on the other hand are a hive intelligence. look at this place, we are already a hive mind here. this is the natural state male minds gravitate towards. necessity for women for sustenance kept men away from something like this, until now. but the future will not be like the things you know.

62382453
>>62377166
Son, for all the bad schizo days you know there are also those days of pure awesome mania...

62382457
>>62376404
They'll be fit only as servitors

62382461
>>62375553
>>62376084
>>62379333
>>62380759
>>62380948
op stfu and just give me the public company to ape into

62382474
>artificial womb

why do you want to be a single parent?

62382691
>>62382461
>op stfu
well now I'm not gonna

62382737
>>62382474
because no woman wants anon. no matter how much money anon makes.

62382774
>>62378763
>Women would still be mothers
Across all the AIslop you generated ITT I just had to laugh at this being a "point" made in a discussion where women would, in fact, no longer be mothers.

62382783
>>62382474
>>62382737
uh, what? I'm talking about an investing opportunity on the business board. I actually just got a message a few minutes ago on fb messenger from one of my sisters friends who's been trying to get with me for a year and my night feels ruined now because it was extremely annoying trying to reject her nicely on the basis of "trust me you do not want anything to do with me, I am autistic and do not like leaving the house" and I still had to ghost her completely. I don't date or have any desire to and I don't want to have kids at all. I am extremely handsome but also an autistic freak who... doesn't like to leave the house kek dating is not for me, I am for looking at only.

62382794
>>62382783
Can I look at your feet?

62382802
>>62382794
no thats gross and gay

62382809
thoughts?

62382812
>>62382802
You're for looking only and you won't even let us look? Fucking prude.

62382814
>>62382774
> new tech would be easily available to public and not only accessible by the wealthy
> the poors and religious fundamentalists wouldn’t continue to multiply like rabbits
Yeah, no more mothers, for sure, good point

62382816
>>62382809
That’s an AI video and very creepy

62382832
>>62376404
Truly horror. The audacity of these humans. It's not just humans they perform horrific and abominable procedures too but animals as well.

>>62377063
Trvke. A horror experiment from decades ago where they removed any sort of affection to a batch of babies. Just mechanical feeding via picking up the kid, no affection, and using a bottle. They quickly started dying off and the experiment ended.

62382834
>>62382814
In the context of "women would still hold their value leveraging this to gain more freedom" and using that as a suggestion of how such women would still hold value...

Yeah nah, the women "leveraging" this to obtain more "freedom" would not be mothers. That wouldn't be the go to source of value to anyone coherent trying to argue birthing was irrelevant to a woman's value. It's ultra retarded.

62382837
>>62375914
Just go somewhere you can hire a prostitute you fucking retard. Then when you realize pussy isn't that special, just buy a quality flesh light and move on with your life.
>>62376404
Thanks Anon, I fully intend to make an entire army of white children this way and your diatribe has made sure I will fight for access during incubation.

62382852
Some of you guys are so fucking stupid that you can not see what is happening right in front of you.

We are long past jerking each other off about the ethics and morality of things....if those ideas had any meaning, we would not allow girls to make more money than a fucking surgeon just by selling her bath water.

OP is fucking right - Robo pussy & artifical wombs is going to fucking send.

This is bitcoin when it was $12.76
This is Amazon when jeff was still living in new mexico selling books
This is Youtube when it had like only 1000 accounts that have registered on the site

I just had an old 55+ year old guy tell me the only reason women have value today is because men want whats between there legs. Throw that out and she's not worth the salt.

The world economic forum stated way back almost a decade ago THAT THIS ALONG WITH GENE-DNA EDITING is going to fucking send.

Think you dumb fuck "If robo pussy is bitcoin at $12.76....what does robo-pussy look like when its at a trillion dollar marketcap?"

62382854
>>62382834
I really want to know what woman ever saw motherhood as a path to freedom. Marriage, yes, but motherhood no.
It’s a desire built into them to want concern, similar to men, but freedom has nothing to do with it. Most women recognize the immediate responsibility and shackling to the child, it’s why motherhood age has increased.

The same women who are trophy wives would continue to be trophy wives because their value was never motherhood.
Haha these threads never cease to crack me up.
Where is this misconception that women lorded babies over men’s heads, and not men lording babies over other men’s heads? They’re the keepers of the babies or some shit not because or an avenue to freedom but because if they pick wrong it’s guaranteed misery

item.title: My x feed has turn into this constantly. — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
My x feed has turn into this constantly.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-18 04:54 UTC

OP

My x feed has turn into this constantly.

1) some "omfg" Claude workflow that will make you 60k/month
2) retards selling courses/coaching/mentorships in how to run a gazillion automated YT/tiktok/whatever pages with screenshots of students making money
3) various clickbait: "MrBeast shares key insights", "This guy just shared to how to make 10k a month with sleep channels", "this guy just made $2,582,285 on a memecoin"

It never ends. And now with AI the effort on having to actually write something catchy isn't there. It's just constant slop with supposed cheat codes to create slop that you can monetize.

The reality is this:
-The slop on the image makes no sense, you cannot replace a Bloomberg terminal with AI because a Bloomberg terminal gets emerging constant new sources that you don't have. Of course im not saying paying for a Bloomberg terminal is going to give you an edge and make you money. But just proving my point. Also all of these "free" workflows always have a paywall somewhere.
-Course selling scammers make money from the courses, this is not news.
-Clickbaiters make money from the traffic.

No one is making money doing something new and cool. By no one I mean, 99% of people. Everything is slop. Anyone making money is doing it from some sort of loophole. It's literally everywhere. There is always something dodgy going on.

For everything cool and genuine (like John Carmack inveting Doom in 1993 and getting rich because the game was just that good and revolutionary) 99% of people that get rich are doing it scaling slop. It will only get worse as AI gets trained on AI generated slop.

And if you try to profit from this, it's just algorithm luck to get your account ranked by the system so your posts get anywhere. Everything is literally a lottery, unless your genius is so good that it eventually thrives even if you are viewthrottled by the algorithm, but that is insanely rare.

Early replies (first 3)

62382855
>>62382838
Its always funny when people for these posts. Why the fuck would someone teach other people on ways of making easy money? Its like people selling trading bots

62382857
>>62382838
You should just stop using X. I rarely even visit this site anymore. Just start building something without all that crap bombarding your mental and you'll be better off. It's all just poison for your time.

62382861
>>62382857
There is no other place to go isn't it? I have another account that I use to find interesting tickers on semis as well as Bitcoin TA takes and basically get a news feed from the walter bloomberg guy. Everyone is on x, where the fuck do you go, facebook?
The thing is, 99% is slop, there's a few good accounts, but most is just monetized slop farms.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382867
maybe unsubscribe from slop?

62382873
>>62382838
top signal

62382878
>>62382867
you can't lol
my xitter exclusively follows gook artists because pixiv sucks but it still bombards me with what is basically ads from grifters

62382884
>>62382861
I don't go anywhere else it's all pretty much the same stuff really. I guess for "socials" I just lurk some sources I like on youtube but for the most part I'm doing my own thing and everything else is just noise. These social platforms like X are all bots and junk there isn't any real substance to any of it.

62382885
>>62382867
Im not subscribed. Even if you unfollow all these accounts, they still show up because they are accounts win tons of followers. Also there's basically no good accounts except such a few ammounts that the feed is just going to get these bigger accounts anyway. Unless you limit it all to the same few accounts you follow, but this way you close yourself to finding something new worth reading.

62382892
>>62382861
Go nowhere, put the time towards slowing down and doing nothing. Honest to god I don’t even miss YouTube anymore. My only social media is Twitch Discord and this place.

62382921
>>62382857
This. X is literally like visiting India. Here is like visiting a boomer's basement to see his millennial son.

62382933
>>62382838
Who cares.

62382980
>>62382884
So you only lurk here?
How is this place any better?
I mean sure, the fact that you cannot make money here is great, you get real people, and the bots have gone down by a lot with the new captcha it seems, but this is still a slop website

62382982
>>62382892
twitch, are we fucking serious? what do you even do there but watch livesloppers
and discord, what channels? I only chat in like 2 places

item.title: > age 34 > drained down to last $20k — 44 replies — 30.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
> age 34 > drained down to last $20k

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-06-18 03:31 UTC

OP

> literally impossible to get just a fucking interview these days
> once had $160k nw (circa 2022) but combination of awful timing decisions with crypto + 4 years unemployed did a toll on me with living expenses
> entrepreneurship is hypersaturated beyond belief, impossible to make money online now
> AI is slaughtering every money making field out there so I can't figure out which masters degree or cert I need to go for next and they're all so fucking expensive too

I'm so scared bros HELP

Early replies (first 3)

62382538
>unemployed 4 years
lmao, imagine being this worthless

62382547
>>62382527
just think if you had bought chip stocks instead of fucking around with crypto you would probably be a millionaire.

62382548
>>62382538
fuck off nigger ur probably some 19 year old shithead u dont know anything life will rape u and humble u faggot
>>62382527
Dont worry about it anon im sure u can find a decent job soon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382593
>>62382583
I meant what I said and I'm implying being a parts delivery guy is beneath the likes of OP, tile requires constant heavy lifting and being hunched down on the floor applying grout I've done tile before.

62382596
>>62382589
barber or have opened a vape shop or tattoo salon and gone from there, plenty of solid ventures at even 10k to start.

62382602
>>62382593
you said go do manual labor. logistics is cushiest of manual labor around.

62382604
>>62382596
He should work as a bartender in the meantime.

62382612
>>62382602
Then OP should be a parts guy instead of trying to make money out of a meme like Entrepreneurship, notice how he mentioned how Ai is taking positions fit for his lazy monkey ass.

62382617
>>62382612
yes and how should he even manage if he can't work in logistics? he should rightly just end it

62382625
>>62382580
cool you could have also put the down payment in nvidia and been retired

62382627
>>62382527
might depend on your area but water treatment plant operators are in demand all over the US
you'll have to work for a shit wage until you get the hours for your license. you mentioned masters degree and this would be easy in comparison, not to mention no debt since most places pay / reimburse
easy 30-40/h after a year spent getting the req hours
I don't live in a major city so I find the pay good, easy to get into because most people smart enough to do it do something else

62382631
>>62382527
>>62382548
Nigga, four years? At a certain point you're just a bum.

62382648
>>62382631
Shit ass job market, 2022 was when the tech bubble started to pop then 2023 AI reared it's ugly head and made everything 100x worse. Fast forward to today and the job market keeps getting worse and worse by the day. For the record I'm not looking for just remote jobs either, and every job has 100+ applicants within the first half hour of posting.

62382653
>>62382648
>Shit ass job market
You had an entire Bachelor's degree of time to figure something out.

62382655
>>62382527
Buy link you dumb faggot

62382660
>>62382625
That wouldn't be enough to retire.

62382662
>>62382631
kek shut up wagie goy cattle

62382669
>>62382655
Buy an ad stinkie

62382670
>>62382662
>goy cattle
You think the Jews are sitting around all day NEETing playing bing bang wahoos?
No nigga they're charging Cletus 30% interest for a 72 month loan on his King Ranch.

62382672
>>62382561
Should've lifted with your knees

62382673
>>62382670
you wouldn't know, best to stay in your lane

62382675
>>62382673
>you wouldn't know
I know more than you.

62382678
>>62382672
>knees don't blow out
>it is scolio
no lifting form could have ever prevented this.

62382681
If not a shill demoralizing thread, get CDL and get a local hourly LTL job. It’s easy money once you learn how to use the truck safely. Also includes just the right amount of physicality so you won’t be a fat ass

62382683
>>62382675
>t. retard.

62382688
>>62382683
I'm right.

62382695
>>62382688
>t.

62382735
>>62382527
if you think you are fucked with 20k left i really hope you run down to 0 so you see how good you actually have it now

62382754
WHO CARES NIGGUH IRAN WON WOOOO

62382755
>>62382548
nah you could have gotten a job in 4 years you're just stupid and will remain poor for life.

62382778
>>62382670
Yeah. Jews literally just Neet and collect welfare. That is unironically what Hasidic jews do.

62382792
>>62382527
>33 years old
>6'3"
>190 pounds
>Agent 47 Chad facial features
>6 pack abs
>$720k net worth
>Live in the middle of nowhere
>Ohio
>Virgin

62382803
>>62382612

>>62382648

item.title: Canadian Mortgages are insane — 6 replies — 21.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Canadian Mortgages are insane

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-18 04:40 UTC

OP

Anyone that bought homes over the last 10 years has no concept of the magnitude of the level of debt they have.

Imagine 400k to 900k mortgages on cookie cutter homes with no space between them. This is just fine in the mind of the average leaf.

Let me be clear, they are never going to pay it off. Never. They think because everyone else has a big ass mortgage that its fine. It took me 17 years just to pay down my 228k mortgage to 68k where it is now. How the fuck do these people think at 38 with a 500k mortgage they can pay it down by the time they retire? LMAO.

Fucking totally disconnected from reality. Millions of us.

Early replies (first 3)

62382768
im sick of this being a thing, just fucking crash already. I have to deal with crashes 100 times a year as a crypto cuck but everyone else in the world with their houses and their gay stocks is just driving off into the sunset. make it make sense nigger

62382788
>>62382765
their retirement plan is your pic

62382793
>>62382788
I find this pick really depressing because it reminds me of my parents. They are just in another fucking world. You cannot convince them that anything is wrong.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382840
>>62382768
Probably because houses and companies have value while crypto is useless and does nothing at all

62382853
>buy bitcoin 2015
>???
>buy house

what was so difficult?

62382958
>>62382765
The sad thing is that the pod in the pic related doesn't even have to say "MAiD" on it for everyone to know what it's supposed to be.
>>62382788
I'm convinced MAID is some kind of scam to prevent wealth transfer from boomers to their kids; there's no way it could enjoy as broad support amongst boomers (leaf boomers are the most selfish on the planet) if that wasn't the case.
Probably the idea is spend everything, leave nothing, and jump in the death pod when you're tapped out.

62378483

>>62375138

Turns out you can't actually run a company by getting lucky with one idea (that was stolen)

62380977

62382772

Meta OS recently had a shitty major release that is so bad that it disabled the in-game music feature. You used to be able to listen to youtube podcasts in GOLF+ and that doesn't even work anymore because Meta broke the integration

62382776
>>62382772
You ever gooned in it?

62382836
>>62375138
They goyim dealt in it

62382889

all their successes now are platforms they just bought like instagram
they do more than people realize but they can't be ranked next to apple and google as sure fire bets anymore imo

item.title: (srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb? — 41 replies — 14.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
(srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb?

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-06-18 02:09 UTC

OP

June 11: chop as market gets ready for pump on spacex trading day

June 12 - June 16: iran FUD is metabolized and market goes up on spacex


June 17 - June 22: FOMC on June 16 EOD causes fud, plus more iran fears

June 22 - Oct 10: NDQ floats upwards 40% while S&P chops and eventually goes up 10%

Oct 10: NDQ reaches a generational top - OpenAI IPOs - Fed announces more hikes due to AI labor inflation - republicans lose congress making trump a lame duck president = no more stimulus grift to pump markets
Oct 10, 2026 - ~Oct 10 2027: as a consequence of the lame duck presidency and the lack of stimulus and the selling of AI venture capital bags onto retail, S&P sells off 40% to round trip all the way back down to 5000

~Oct 10 2027: anthropic ipos

~Oct 10 2027 onwards: pretty much a new 20 year cycle begins and most likely the end of humanity as we birth sentience of machines.

Early replies (first 3)

62349969
you need to factor in the wc which is the biggest npc bread & circus event in modern history

62349972
>>62349960
i just matched up the timelines for 1973 and 2000 btw, that's where I'm getting the play by play for things. I reckon the trading AI's are probably averaging the two together given the macro environment

62349980
>>62349969
ah good point, ya so that might erase the june 17 - june 22 fud period and we go straight into june 22 - oct 10 period

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359749
>>62359675
what do you want to hear? the market will go up or go down. your doom pronouncement is stupid since people have been predicting the apocalypse for thousands of years and have always been wrong. also LLMs are gay and AI companies deserve to be buried in piracy and antitrust lawsuits by the next administration. I hope every AI ceo ends up in jail

62359780
>>62359708
Sex with this anon

62359931
>>62359749
fuck yourself, im gonna keep snailposting till you retards give me some concrete nostradamus dates and counter reasoning exactly like you retards do on the 1pbtid pepe threads

62359962
Line will continue up until 7/4/28
7*4 = 28 and they get the satisfaction of destroying a holiday. Will also hand everything to the dems in the next election.

62359991
>>62349960
This is now a Flielen thread

62359998
>>62359991

62361834
snailposting for continued feedback, you're not getting rid of me that easily

62361911
>>62349960
Woah wait a second Frieren, the FOMC is next week? Fucccccccckkkk I need to deleverage.
>FOMC -> NDQ goes up
Walk me through this part if they hike wouldn't that cause a dump or something not more pumping? So should I hold my bullish positions or not? Also I'm pretty sure both Anthropic and OpenAI ipo this year in that order.

62361941
>>62353866

not sure if that counts

62363423
>>62361911
when trump said he'll be signing the agreement on sunday, the target rates dumped

https://www.cmegroup.com/markets/interest-rates/cme-fedwatch-tool.html

warsh is gonna come out next week and be dovish, but the market will act like its not simply because they're range trading, and they're gonna rebuy their positions.

62364277
motheryuckers, this snail post will exist for years

62364284
>>62349960
>metabolized
Stop reading right there.

62366455
>>62364284

don't worry, illiterate retard opinions aren't needed

62366592
>>62366455
I didn't give an opinion.

62368882
snailposting till nostradamus pomp or domp predictions

62370523
snailposting is just transient guys

62370546
>>62370523
Reddit elf

62370557
>>62370546
tard

62370592
>>62370523

How certain are you about that November election? Rs literally gerrymandered the south and killed D seats + added 12. Trump picks have won every primary solidifying his control. I think it will be a narrow rig in order for the country to not go "troubles" Dem or Rep doesn't fucking matter, probably Rep +1 in the house

62372434
>>62370592
hmm good point, but there's no way they can unfuck the public opinion by november. The pain of inflation will be too fresh, and the memory of Iran causing that pain to boot. All the poor people will be out in force

62372555
>>62359780
Is Frieren a virgin?

Thanks for this thread OP

62375431
>>62372555
i am a virgin, yes, i am frieren btw, you're welcome

62378224
i am goy cattle slave

anyway, let's see what warsh has to say tomorrow. I'm guessing it'll be exactly what everyone expects, and the market will dump, because "oh my god it's what we expected therefore we must domp eeet" and then news headlines will make up some bullshit about it not being exactly what everyone expected

then we'll start our final ascent next week

the alternative is as the other anon said and the WC just completely eats any remaining fud and we begin the ascent tomorrow.

idk call it

62378317
>>62349960
I'll play along; assuming you're 100% correct: how do I benefit the most from this timeline?

62378908
>>62378317

For the next leg, just buy a portfolio of ndq & btc, maybe hype too if you're close to arthur and have advanced notice of when their gang is gonna commence_ze_domp.exe. It'd be hard to pick a specific time + stock individually due to how many of them have been bid up already, I think it'll just be ndq as a whole that floats upwards carried by the larger ai stocks getting bid up on anthropic bullish biz announcements


At the top, take out far OTM 3-6 month puts on the stocks way out on the risk curve of AI. You can probably just ask claude or chatgpt to come up with a list, but this list was decent (not great, but ok):

https://www.reddit.com/r/wallstreetbets/comments/1tuwp73/in_the_ai_gold_rush_what_are_the_shovels/

close the puts once spy/ndq are around 15% off the top, nearing the "bear market" 20% fud line where the money printers tend to start spinning

At the 20% fud line, short vix futures


after the initial leg down, you can either assume gold/btc/commodities will do well (like they did in 1973) or continue to be suppressed with the market's decline (like they did in 2000). If they're suppressed, then just scalp spy on each retrace and short it at the top of each retrace (whatever vehicle works here). If they're not suppressed, I'd just buy gld/btc/commodities and capture long term cap gains and chill


just a footnote: the reason I say 3-6 months is because of trump, if he wasn't president, I'd just do 1-2 month. If you feel he won't pull some bullshit, then ya go for it

risk size is whatever you want, don't use this post to construct your reason for suicide pls

62378916
>>62349960
Problem with posts like these is they make the predictions way too fast. Put the 2027 prediction in 2037 and yeah sure.

Remember, "nothing ever happens"

62378940
>>62378317
also, the full retard method for June 22 - Oct 10 assuming im 100% correct would be spcx, but i have no insight to their insiders

62380718
https://www.financialjuice.com/News/9640328/The-US-and-Iran-discuss-moving-up-the-signing-of-the-deal---Axios-citing-sources-.aspx

*bzzzz bzzzzz* "bogdanoff, he panic sold"

"pomp eet"

I guess trump got whatever negotiations with musk and altman in place, or maybe not, or maybe yes, or maybe not, or maybe yes

62381248
https://www.financialjuice.com/News/9640622/Fed-9-of-18-FOMC-participants-pencil-in-a-2026-rate-hike.aspx

*bzzzzt bzzzzzt* "bogdanoff, he bot ze dip"

"domp eet"

>>62378224


> then news headlines will make up some bullshit about it not being exactly what everyone expected

"warsh betrayed us"

62382661
snailpost

XLM will be pegged to the price of silver (for retail)

XRP can go as high as $37500 once it's fully used

OP

>Bought the tippy top of Gold/Silver when normies were posting themselves lining up in droves to buy shiny boomer rocks on social media.

OP

I wanna buy stocks.

62377467
>>62377463

62377469
>>62377463

1k doesnt do anything

62377486

good luck

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62377547
>>62377463
Buy things tech relies on and are in critically short supply. Elon isn't going to Mars if he can't get tungsten, electricity or fuel. Find exports china will turn off in its economic war against the US. Or though it all into NVDA knowing the entire economy is currently relying on the market just ignoring said bottlenecks away forever.

>>62377463

62377980
>>62377463

62377985
>>62377980

62377986

62378011
If you are under 40 pick an index fund like ITOT OR IVV and put a thousand in every time you have it.

Or put in whatever you have at the end of the week or month.

Or listen to the smart traders on here a YOLO into whatever they like and dock your yacht beside theirs and be sure to show up with the finest wine because that’s the least you can do for someone who gave you and really wants to give you the hottest tip. Wear something pretty!
